anyOf.description: A single MCP server configuration.
Arguments passed to the command. Each argument may use env expansion.
Arguments passed to the command. Each argument may use env expansion.

args.description: Arguments passed to the command. Each argument may use env expansion.

command.description: Executable used to start the MCP server. Supports env expansion, e.g. "${HOME}/bin/server".

anyOf.description: Optional human-readable description of this server. Not currently documented, but useful for tooling/UX.
Optional human-readable description of this server. Not currently documented, but useful for tooling/UX.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of this server. Not currently documented, but useful for tooling/UX.

anyOf.description:
Environment variables to set *for the server process / HTTP client*.

Values support environment expansion:
  - "${VAR}" - required env var VAR
 - "${VAR:-default}" → VAR if set, otherwise literal "default"

For HTTP servers, these are process-level env vars (not HTTP headers).
For stdio servers, they become process env vars.

Environment variables to set *for the server process / HTTP client*.

Values support environment expansion:
  - "${VAR}" - required env var VAR
 - "${VAR:-default}" → VAR if set, otherwise literal "default"

For HTTP servers, these are process-level env vars (not HTTP headers).
For stdio servers, they become process env vars.

env.description:
Environment variables to set *for the server process / HTTP client*.

Values support environment expansion:
  - "${VAR}" - required env var VAR
 - "${VAR:-default}" → VAR if set, otherwise literal "default"

For HTTP servers, these are process-level env vars (not HTTP headers).
For stdio servers, they become process env vars.

anyOf.description: Optional OAuth configuration for this server. Only meaningful for HTTP/SSE transports.

Override for the OAuth metadata discovery URL.

When the server’s standard
  /.well-known/oauth-authorization-server
endpoint is broken, Claude can be configured to fetch metadata
from a specific OIDC configuration URL instead.

Must be an https:// URL according to docs.

Example:

 "authServerMetadataUrl":
   "https://auth.example.com/.well-known/openid-configuration"

anyOf.description:
Fixed callback port used for the redirect URI:
  http://localhost:<callbackPort>/callback

Used when the OAuth app has a pre-registered redirect URI
with a fixed port.

Fixed callback port used for the redirect URI:
  http://localhost:<callbackPort>/callback

Used when the OAuth app has a pre-registered redirect URI
with a fixed port.

callbackPort.description:
Fixed callback port used for the redirect URI:
  http://localhost:<callbackPort>/callback

Used when the OAuth app has a pre-registered redirect URI
with a fixed port.

anyOf.description:
OAuth client ID.

Typically configured either:
 - via CLI flags (`--client-id`), or
 - directly in `.mcp.json`.

May be required depending on the server.

mcpServers.description: The top-level MCP server configurations keyed by server name. The key is the logical server name (e.g. "github", "sentry", "internal-db").
